title: swertia albomarginata subsp. purpusii
intro: Swertia albomarginata subsp. purpusii, also known as a type of alpine gentian, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its white-edged leaves and purple flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat a variety of ailments. The health benefits of Swertia albomarginata subsp. purpusii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain and inflammation, and protect against infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
